steps.title: Identify the outcome the sediments must produce
steps.content: The phrase “eventually forming new portions of land” signals what the sediments' action must accomplish—this is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Match the word to the outcome
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean gather and pile up — an action that leads to forming new portions of land where the river meets the Gulf. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Accumulate: "sediments accumulate where the river meets the Gulf" — accumulate means collect and build up over time, directly producing new land. ✓
  • Choice B — Dissolve: "sediments dissolve where the river meets the Gulf" — dissolve means break apart into liquid; dissolved sediments cannot form new land. ✗
  • Choice C — Accelerate: "sediments accelerate where the river meets the Gulf" — accelerate means speed up; sediments speeding up cannot explain the formation of new land. ✗
  • Choice D — Thrive: "sediments thrive where the river meets the Gulf" — thrive means flourish or prosper, a term for living things, not inorganic sediments. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
